summary: PR #2262 decompiled six fighter functions across Kirby, Popo, and Crazy Hand source files. The PR body claimed clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, and the bot report recorded six new 100% matches totaling +652 matched-code bytes plus a small .sdata2 improvement.

key_files.role: Replaced ftCh_Slap_Anim placeholder with a matched animation-transition function.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftCh_Slap_Anim 0.00% to 100.00%, +116 bytes

key_files.role: Replaced two large Kirby neutral-special collision placeholders with matched ground/air transition handlers.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists both functions 0.00% to 100.00%, +116 bytes each

key_files.role: Replaced ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 placeholder with a matched helper that computes an effect position from a fighter part joint or clears the output vector on null input.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 0.00% to 100.00%, +112 bytes

key_files.role: Replaced ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC placeholder with a matched Mewtwo-copy motion-state predicate using a grouped switch.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC 0.00% to 100.00%, +84 bytes

key_files.role: Replaced ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 placeholder with a matched cleanup/reset function for a Popo special state flag, effects, callbacks, and part rotation.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 0.00% to 100.00%, +108 bytes

what_changed: Six placeholder comments were replaced with C implementations: ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00, ftKb_LgSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_LgSpecialAirN_Coll, ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898, and ftCh_Slap_Anim.

Kirby large neutral-special collision handlers now switch between ground and air motion states using ft_80082708 or ft_80081D0C, call ftCommon ground/air transition helpers, preserve fp->cur_anim_frame, and restore accessory4_cb to fn_800F98F4.
Kirby Mewtwo-copy helper ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC now returns false for eight listed Mewtwo-copy start/loop/full/end ground and air motion IDs and true otherwise, including null gobj.

Kirby Pikachu-copy effect helper ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 now guards gobj and fp, uses GET_FIGHTER, computes an output Vec3 through lb_8000B1CC from fp->parts[44].joint with offset {0.0f, 1.45f, 5.016f}, and zeros the output vector on null input.

Popo helper ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 now checks fp->fv.pp.x2230_b0, destroys effects, clears the flag and death/damage callbacks, and resets part rotation with ftPartSetRotX; the implementation includes PAD_STACK(16).
Crazy Hand ftCh_Slap_Anim now waits until no animation frames remain, changes to motion state 0x15D, calls ftAnim_8006EBA4, and restores accessory4_cb to fn_801577B4.

smart_moves.text: Used direct Fighter fields and existing helpers rather than raw pointer arithmetic for state, callbacks, current animation frame, and fighter vars.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: uses fp->cur_anim_frame, fp->accessory4_cb, fp->fv.pp.x2230_b0, fp->death2_cb, fp->take_dmg_cb

smart_moves.text: Grouped related motion IDs in a switch for ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC instead of a long branch ladder.
Kept the PAD_STACK use narrow to one matched Popo function rather than applying broad stack steering across the file.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16) appears only in ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 excerpt
text_corpus: bot report lists ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 as 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fighter callback and collision functions can often match with straightforward existing helper calls and typed Fighter fields: motion-state changes, ground/air transition helpers, callback restoration, and fighter vars carried most of this PR.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report six new 100% matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: For predicate-style motion checks, a grouped switch over motion_id can be a clean, matchable source form when several adjacent named motion states share the same return behavior.
When a helper writes an output Vec3, preserving explicit null guards and field-by-field fallback zeroing may be part of the matching source shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 null checks and vec->z/y/x zeroing

naming_conventions.convention: Address-suffixed or address-style function names were retained where semantic names were not introduced by the PR: ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00, ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898, fn_800F98F4, and fn_801577B4.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: listed matched functions retain address-style names
raw_diff: implementations call fn_800F98F4 and fn_801577B4

naming_conventions.convention: Local fighter object pointer is named gobj and Fighter pointer is named fp, consistent with common fighter-source shorthand visible in the diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: all new functions use HSD_GObj* or Fighter_GObj* gobj and Fighter* fp
text_corpus: bot report ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898 0.00% to 100.00%, +108 bytes
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence supports that this function matched with PAD_STACK present, but the slice does not include discussion explaining why the stack pad was necessary or whether alternatives were tried.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used grouped switch cases over named motion IDs for ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This aligns with the loaded canonical-control-flow-and-macros standard, but no reviewer discussion is present.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used typed field access and existing project helpers instead of generated offset math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GET_FIGHTER, gobj->user_data, fp->parts[44].joint, fp->fv.pp.x2230_b0, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftCommon_8007D5D4, ftCommon_8007D7FC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The match report supports the resulting functions as 100%, but the slice does not show the decompiler output or rejected alternatives.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merged state, author, timestamps, changed-file list
human_pr_text: PR summary and claimed verification
raw_diff: complete 4465-byte diff for five changed files

notes: No review comments or reviews are present in the slice, so reviewer intent and review feedback cannot be inferred.
The PR body claims ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0; the bot report independently records six new 100% matches, but no full build log or objdiff artifacts are loaded.
The raw PR JSON is truncated, but the loaded human_pr_text, raw_diff, changed_files, and text_corpus are sufficient for this compact intake.
The PR body says the code was generated with Claude Code; this is recorded as PR text evidence only and not treated as proof of authored intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Grouped motion-id switch matched a fighter predicate
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2262, ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106FEC matched at 100% using a switch over fp->motion_id with grouped Mewtwo-copy motion-state cases returning false and default/null paths returning true. This is a concrete example supporting ordinary switch forms for adjacent state predicates.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed fighter fields and helpers were enough for several 100% fighter matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2262 matched six small fighter functions while using gobj->user_data or GET_FIGHTER, Fighter fields, fighter-var fields, and existing helpers such as Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftCommon_8007D5D4, ftCommon_8007D7FC, efLib_DestroyAll, ftPartSetRotX, and lb_8000B1CC.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report six new 100% matches

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK example with narrow match evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2262 provides an example where PAD_STACK(16) appears in a single matched fighter function, ftPp_SpecialHi_80122898, and the bot report records that function as a new 100% match. The slice does not explain why the pad was necessary, so this should only be considered as an example under the existing matching-tactics evidence standard, not as a general recommendation.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could enrich the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard with a concrete, narrowly evidenced historical example while preserving that PAD_STACK requires justification.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'Claude Code generated' as a decomp method or accepted source style; it is only present in the PR body footer.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale because the slice has no review comments or reviews.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK as acceptable style from this PR alone; the slice lacks explanation of necessity or alternative attempts.
Do not claim all six functions were objdiff-verified from loaded artifacts; the available evidence is PR-body verification text plus decomp-dev bot match percentages.
